name: ez barbell seated curls
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Rest your upper arms on your thighs, allowing the ez barbell to hang down in front of you.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the ez barbell upwards towards your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the ez barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight. Hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your hands shoulder-width apart. Rest your upper arms on your thighs, allowing the ez barbell to hang down in front of you. Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the ez barbell upwards towards your shoulders. Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps. Inhale and slowly lower the ez barbell back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
